title: salix iliensis
intro: Salix iliensis, also known as Iliamna willow or Iliamna cottonwood, is a species of willow native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its gray bark and cottony seeds. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits of Salix iliensis include reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating menstrual cramps, making it a popular herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of Salix iliensis often involve infusing the bark or leaves in hot water to make a tea or tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
